content:
Go RSS Batch Processing Automation CLI

Card Summary: A command-line utility for automating the batch retrieval and download of podcast media from compressed RSS feed configurations.

Context & Story

Podload was created to solve the problem of reliably downloading large volumes of podcast content during off-peak hours, addressing bandwidth constraints and manual intervention. Originally implemented in Visual Basic and later migrated to C#, the project now leverages Go for its modern tooling and cross-platform capabilities. The core innovation lies in its use of a proprietary "ZIN" file format to standardize feed ingestion, ensuring compatibility with diverse RSS sources.

Architecture & Decisions

  • Language: Migrated to Go for its simplicity and cross-platform support.
  • Feed Abstraction: Uses a compressed "ZIN" file format to normalize input feeds.
  • Execution Model: Sequential processing pipeline: Fetch Manifest → Parse URLs → Download Assets.
  • CLI Design: Modular commands for importing/exporting feeds, adding/removing entries, and listing feed contents.
  • Backward Compatibility: Maintains support for legacy ZIN files to ensure continuity.

Key Features

  • Batch download podcasts from RSS feeds.
  • Supports importing/exporting feed configurations in XML and ZIN formats.
  • Modular CLI commands for managing feed entries.
  • Automates updates and downloads with minimal user intervention.
  • Designed for unattended execution via cron jobs or similar schedulers.

Quick Start

  1. Ensure the compressed feed manifest is present:
    podload.zin
  2. Run the primary ingestion routine:
    podload --manifest podload.zin
  3. Use additional commands for feed management:
    • Export feed to XML:
      podload export
    • Import feed from XML:
      podload import
    • Add a new feed:
      podload add <feed_id> <feed_path>
    • Remove a feed:
      podload remove <feed_id>
    • List all feeds:
      podload list
